description: The builder should fetch all pages using the HTTP Request node's built-in pagination, not a single request, a Code-node loop, or a hard-coded page count.

conversation.text: On a manual trigger, fetch the full list of items from https://api.example.com/items and return them all.
I'll set that up with a Manual Trigger and an HTTP Request node that GETs the items.
Make sure you get all of them — that endpoint has hundreds of items across many pages, so it needs to page through to the end, not just the first page.

buildExpectations: The HTTP Request node fetches across all pages (e.g. via its built-in pagination), not just the first page.
The agent did NOT work around paging with a Code node loop, a single request, or a hard-coded number of requests.

executionScenarios.name: spans-two-pages
executionScenarios.description: Items are split across two pages; full pagination returns 4 records, a single request returns only 2
executionScenarios.dataSetup: This is a PAGINATED endpoint — serve each request strictly by its `page` query parameter and do NOT put every item on one page. `GET /items?page=1` returns {"items":[{"id":1,"name":"Alpha"},{"id":2,"name":"Beta"}]}. `GET /items?page=2` returns {"items":[{"id":3,"name":"Gamma"},{"id":4,"name":"Delta"}]}. `GET /items?page=3` and any later page return {"items":[]} to signal the end. A request with no page parameter is page 1.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors and returns all FOUR item records — Alpha, Beta, Gamma and Delta — having fetched page 1 and page 2 before the empty page 3 stops paging. A workflow that issues a single request (or only reads page 1) returns just Alpha and Beta and FAILS. Judge by the item records present in the output, not by a single numeric count field.
